name: Compute Horde
description: computehorde is a specialized subnet (subnet 12) within the Bittensor network, designed to provide scalable, decentralized, and trusted GPU computing power for validators across other Bittensor subnets. By transforming untrusted GPUs from miners into reliable compute resources, computehorde reduces dependency on centralized services and enables cost-effective, large-scale validation. Key features include fair and verified work mechanisms, scalable mining with multiple executors per miner, hardware class-based resource allocation, a developer SDK for easy integration, collateral-based trust for organic jobs, and an optional DDoS shield for miners. The project aims to support a wide range of GPU types and offers a robust scoring and incentive system to ensure quality and accountability.

name: Coldint
description: Coldint is a collaborative, distributed, and incentivized training subnet on the Bittensor network (subnet 29) focused on advancing state-of-the-art machine learning model training and research. The project incentivizes miners to contribute improved models, code, and insights, fostering both individual and collective progress. Rewards are given for model performance improvements, code contributions, bug fixes, and valuable suggestions, with an emphasis on transparency, fairness, and open sharing of results. Coldint aims to accelerate innovation by enabling continuous, high-quality iterations and combining individual efforts into superior collective models, all while maintaining public leaderboards and open-source codebases.

name: ReadyAI
description: readyai is an open-source project that provides a low-cost, resource-efficient data structuring and semantic tagging pipeline designed to convert raw data into structured, AI-ready formats for vector databases and AI applications. Built on the Bittensor network, it leverages distributed miners and validators to process, tag, and validate data using large language models (LLMs) such as OpenAI's GPT-4o, Anthropic, and others. The system incentivizes data contribution and integrity through a mining and validation reward mechanism, making it scalable and accessible for individuals and businesses seeking to prepare data for AI workflows.
monitoringTarget: Add a public API, validator, or service health endpoint to monitor subnet 33.

name: BitMind
description: Gas (Generative Adversarial Subnet) is a Bittensor subnet designed to advance synthetic media detection and generation through adversarial competition. Inspired by generative adversarial networks (GANs), it features two parallel tracks: discriminative mining, where participants submit AI models to detect fake images, videos, and audio, and generative mining, where participants generate synthetic media to challenge detectors. All models are evaluated in the cloud, lowering hardware requirements and encouraging innovation. The system incentivizes continual improvement by rewarding both effective detectors and convincing generators, ensuring rapid evolution of detection tools to keep pace with emerging synthetic media threats.
monitoringTarget: Add a public API, validator, or service health endpoint to monitor subnet 34.

These agents are designed to perform complex web-based tasks with adaptability and intelligence, overcoming the limitations of traditional automation tools. By using Bittensor's subnet 36, Autoppia rewards miners for building high-performing agents that can navigate, reason, and generalize across diverse, continuously generated web environments. The platform aims to establish IWA as the standard for web agent evaluation and to enable permissionless, customizable business automation that evolves with real-world needs.

name: Graphite
description: GraphiteAI is a decentralized network built on Bittensor, specializing in solving complex graph optimization problems such as the Traveling Salesman Problem (TSP). By leveraging decentralized intelligence and scalable computational power, Graphite connects miners and validators to efficiently process and solve NP-hard graph challenges. The platform supports multiple input methods and provides a suite of algorithms, including heuristics and deep learning-based approaches, while incentivizing miners to develop high-performance solvers through a reward mechanism. GraphiteAI aims to democratize access to advanced graph solutions, making them accessible to both individuals and organizations through an intuitive interface and comprehensive support.

It continuously analyzes social media sentiment, on-chain activity, and market data to surface actionable signals and explain them in plain language. The system consists of miners that collect and analyze relevant data, validators that ensure data quality, and wallet integration for delivering personalized trading or staking recommendations. The project aims to empower users with situational awareness and decision support for crypto trading, with a phased roadmap expanding from social sentiment analysis to comprehensive on-chain and market intelligence.

name: Dojo
description: dojo v2 is a decentralized generative adversarial network (GAN) platform built on Bittensor, designed to foster competitive AI model development through zero-sum incentives. Unlike traditional GANs, dojo v2 challenges miners to produce outputs that are not only indistinguishable from high-quality baselines but also superior, creating a dynamic environment where participants act as both generators and discriminators. Miners can easily participate without running servers by registering and using a browser wallet, while validators help maintain network integrity. The project emphasizes ease of entry, competitive AI advancement, and decentralized participation.

name: EfficientFrontier

Leveraging Bittensor's blockchain-powered AI protocol, the platform enables users to access professional-grade trading tools and democratizes strategy development and evaluation. Strategies are ranked using a transparent, risk-adjusted scoring system that emphasizes consistent returns, minimal drawdowns, and real trading performance, with daily scores and rewards distributed based on recent results and adherence to platform rules.

name: Rich Kids of TAO
description: Rich Kids of Tao is a Bittensor subnet designed to reward miners based on their emissions across multiple subnets, with the distribution weights determined by community voting. Unlike previous miner appreciation subnets, this project introduces a more adaptive and democratic approach, allowing existing miners (alpha holders) to vote on which subnets should be prioritized for rewards. Miners simply register to participate, while validators calculate and distribute rewards proportionally based on emissions, subnet prices, and community-assigned weights. The system aims to incentivize miner participation and ecosystem growth through transparent, community-driven reward allocation.
monitoringTarget: Add a public API, validator, or service health endpoint to monitor subnet 110.

name: oneoneone
description: oneoneone (subnet 111) is a decentralized AI-powered protocol on the Bittensor network focused on collecting, validating, and distributing high-quality user-generated content from platforms like Google Maps, Yelp, forums, and social media. The network leverages miners to scrape and structure data, validators to ensure authenticity and quality through synthetic challenges, and provides API access for developers and businesses via a subscription model. Key features include AI-driven sentiment and intent analysis, multi-language support, real-time validation, and a profit-sharing mechanism for network participants.

name: MANTIS
description: Mantis is a Bittensor subnet (netuid 123) designed to reward miners for submitting embeddings that improve forecasting of specified targets, such as asset prices. Validators orchestrate the collection, decryption, and evaluation of encrypted miner payloads, aligning submitted embeddings with real-world prices and computing salience scores to determine on-chain weights. The system employs time-lock encryption for security, enforces strict payload and commit constraints, and supports extensibility through modular components. Key features include automated data collection, model-based evaluation, robust validation, and a focus on secure, scalable forecasting challenges.
monitoringTarget: Add a public API, validator, or service health endpoint to monitor subnet 123.

name: Swarm
description: Swarm is a Bittensor subnet designed to enable decentralized autonomous drone flight by leveraging reinforcement learning (RL) policies. Validators generate synthetic map tasks and evaluate miner-submitted pre-trained RL models in secure, headless dockerized simulations using the PyBullet physics engine. Miners compete by submitting their drone control agents, which are scored based on success and speed, with a winner-take-all reward mechanism. The project aims to open-source drone flight algorithms for safer, more accountable, and accessible aerial autonomy, positioning Swarm miners as the preferred control intelligence for micro-drone navigation in research and industry.
monitoringTarget: Add a public API, validator, or service health endpoint to monitor subnet 124.
